terms.definition: A generated file recording the exact resolved versions of all dependencies (direct and transitive), often with hashes, for deterministic installs.
The guarantee that installing a project's dependencies yields the identical set every time and on every machine.
A package required by one of your dependencies rather than declared directly by you.
Fixing a dependency to an exact version (and optionally a hash) so it cannot change unexpectedly.
Recording a cryptographic checksum per package so installs verify artifact integrity.
The hand-authored declaration of loose dependency constraints (e.g. requirements.txt or pyproject.toml) from which a lock is resolved.
